description_long: This robot dog features a compact, quadrupedal design with a rectangular, lime-green main body and four articulated legs. The front legs are light gray with visible joint segments, while the rear legs are black, giving a striking two-tone appearance. The head area is integrated into the front of the body, with what appears to be a sensor or camera array for navigation and interaction. The legs are equipped with small, paw-like feet, likely designed for stability and traction on urban surfaces. The body includes several mechanical joints and possible actuator housings, suggesting a high degree of mobility and flexibility. The overall build is robust, with a mix of metallic and polymer materials, suitable for navigating streets, sidewalks, and other urban environments.
description: A compact, lime-green and black robot dog with articulated legs and a sensor-equipped head, designed for urban mobility.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sensor array and symmetrical leg positioning, with the lime-green body and gray front legs most visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the segmented, articulated legs and the contrast between the gray front and black rear legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the black rear legs and the back of the main body, with less detail on the head or sensors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same two-tone leg design and the mechanical structure of the limbs.
